folio.label: 6r
folio.l: xxx thxugh a wydewe , had nou©t a shoo
So plesant was his , In Principio

note.notetxt: Principio256the indication of the capital is in the fact that the P has a small vertical line in the loop.ODR

folio.l: ñet wold he haue , a ferthyng or he went
His p¼chas was wel betir , than his rent
And rage he coude , as it had be a whelp
In louedayes , there coude he meche help
For there he was , nat lyke a cloisterere
W a thredbare Cope , as is a pore scolere
But he was like a maistä , or a Pope
Of double worstede , was his semy Cope
Rounde , as any belle , ou©t of the presse
Som what he lisped , for his wantounesse
To make his englyssú , swete on his tonge
And in his harpyng , whan that he songe
Hys eyen twynkeled , in his hede a rigút
As don the sterres , in a frosty nygút
This worthy lymytoå , was cleped Huber´
A Marchunt æere was , w a forked berd´
In motlee , and heye on hors he satte
And on his heued , a flaundryssú beuö hatte
His botes clasped , faire and fetisly
His resons he spak , ful solempnely
Sownyng alwey , the encres of his wynnyng´
He wold æe see were kepte , for any thyng´
By twene midelburgú and Orewelle

note.notetxt: midelburgú279 m is not a capital letter and has the first minim traced as a secretary form, continuous. The third minim is traced separatelly.ODR

folio.l: Wel coude he , in eschaunges sheldes selle
This worthy man , ful wel , his wyt bysette
There wyst no wyght , that he was in dette
So stedefastly dede he , of his gouänance
W his bargeyns , and w his cheuysance
For sothe he was , a worthy man w alle
But sothe to seyn , I ne wot how men hi~ calle
A Clerk ther was , of Oxenfor´ also
That vnto logyk´ , had longe I go
And lene was his hors , as it were a rake
And he was nou©t right fat , I vndertake
But loked holwe , and ther to soberly
Ful thredbare was his ouäest Courteby
For he had ©et , geten hi~ no benefice
Ne was nou©t wordly , to haue an Office
For hi~ was leuä , haue , at his beddis hede
